text:
CORVALLIS Ore. (KPTV) - Olympic fever is running high in Beaver Nation.

Oregon State’s Jade Carey is back in the States after earning a pair of gymnastics medals at the Paris games.

OSU welcomed Carey back home on Wednesday with a parade through Parker Plaza. They’re pretty dam proud of Carey in Beaver Nation.

“This community is more than I ever could have asked for so I just can’t thank everyone enough for always being there, it definitely doesn’t go unnoticed and I appreciate it a lot,” said gold and bronze medalist for Team USA Jade Carey.

SEE ALSO:

Carey’s return flight out of France back to the Beaver State came with two prized carry-ons back to Corvallis, individual bronze and team gold.

“I was super excited to be a part of the team this time. It’s truly one of the best experiences of my life. It’s something that I’ve always wanted,” said Carey.

The 24-year-old from Phoenix is the most decorated gymnast in OSU history, shining on the global stage despite battling a stomach bug.

“It was probably a lot harder than it looked,” Carey laughs. “There were definitely days where I didn’t think that I could do it or I should just stop because I just didn’t feel at my best.”

Her podium performance in the vault was redemption for her stumble in the same event during the 2021 games in Tokyo.

“I’ve anticipated having that re-do for three years now so it was kind of just like, don’t mess up, be normal,” said Carey.

With Carey’s Team USA gold around her neck, it adds to the gold she won on the floor when she was solo in Japan.

The senior in digital communication arts embarks on the fall quarter in late Sept., but first get some time to relax and enjoy all the moments.

“I grew up in the gym from the minute I was born pretty much but I just remember when I was a kid just running around crazy, copying all the older girls,” said Carey. “Trying to do what they were doing, probably scaring my dad half to death but just exploring like that and having fun has been the biggest part of my journey. I’ve always had fun.”

SEE ALSO:

Just ask her little fans who dream big just like Carey did.

FOX 12′s Nick Krupke asked the young fans, “What do you like that Jade does?”

One kid said, " That I do it too,” another, “Sometimes we actually try to copy her.”

“You want to be just like her?” asked Nick.

To which they agree, “Yeah.”

Carey is being shown love by the Beaver Nation Wednesday and forever as she will go down in history as one of the greats. She says she’ll be back for the 2025 Beaver gymnastics season in Gill Coliseum.

title: OSU gymnast Jade Carey back from Paris Olympics with gold and bronze medals
description: OSU gymnast comes back from Paris Olympics with gold and bronze.
